title: Senior Manager, Social
pay_candidate_snippets: Simple Mills was also a 2020 Health Magazine Snack Award Winner!
raw_description: Company Description Simple Mills is on a mission to transform the way food is made—creating nourishing products with astounding ingredients and amazing taste. Founded in 2012 by Katlin Smith, our brand has redefined center-aisle grocery by delivering better-for-you products made with unique, nutrient-dense ingredients. Today, we’re a category leader and the #1 natural brand in multiple segments, with distribution in over 30, stores nationwide. Now, as part of Flowers Foods, we’re entering an exciting new phase of growth—expanding our reach, accelerating innovation, and amplifying brand awareness, all while preserving the integrity of what makes Simple Mills special. Our commitment to using simple, high-quality ingredients ensures that every bite is both delicious and nutritious. Whether it’s watermelon seed flour in our cookies or butternut squash in our crackers, we craft our products with innovative, wholesome ingredients that deliver extraordinary taste. Our work has been recognized by Fast Company as a Brand That Matters and a Most Innovative Company, and has been featured in Vogue, Forbes, Women’s Health, and NPR’s How I Built This. In 2024, Inc. named us one of its Best Workplaces, reflecting our dedication to fostering an inspiring and inclusive environment where passionate individuals can make a difference. Join us as we shape the future of food. at www.simplemills.com. Role Description The Senior Manager, Social will play a pivotal role in shaping how Simple Mills shows up in culture, leading the -day strategy and execution of our organic social presence across platforms. This individual will translate brand and business priorities into a clear, differentiated social approach, guiding content direction and partnering closely with agency teams to deliver consistent, high-quality output. As a key member of the Integrated Marketing team, this role operates as a thought partner to leadership, contributing to direction while owning execution, performance, and continuous optimization. The ideal candidate is both creatively instinctive and analytically grounded, with a strong point of view on content, platforms, and what drives engagement. They are deeply attuned to cultural and consumer conversations, and able to translate trends in health, wellness, and sustainability into relevant, social-native storytelling. This role reports to the Senior Director, Integrated Marketing and responsibilities will include: Social Strategy & Channel Leadership - Shape and refine Simple Mills’ organic social strategy across Instagram, TikTok,, and (with Pinterest, YouTube, X, and Threads as secondary platforms). - Translate integrated marketing plans into platform-specific strategies, content plans, and success measures, ensuring clarity in what each channel is driving. - Develop TikTok Shop strategy and execution in partnership with external partners, ensuring alignment to broader social and business goals. - Identify and test opportunities to evolve channel approach, including new formats, tools, and platform capabilities, bringing forward data-informed recommendations. - Partner closely with Brand and Integrated Marketing teams to support launches, retail moments, influencers, and key initiatives. Content & Creative Direction - Lead content development in partnership with agency, ensuring output is strategic, insight-driven, and aligned to platform best practices, with a balance of hands-on input and directional leadership. - Guide development of monthly content calendars and production workflows, ensuring clarity in priorities, timelines, and deliverables (~40–50 assets/month). - Contribute to creative direction, developing repeatable formats, series, and storytelling approaches that build engagement over time. - Develop social-native creative ideas and content series in partnership with agency and influencers/creators that bring the Simple Mills brand and mission to life, translating complex topics (health, wellness, sustainability) into clear, engaging storytelling across formats. - Ensure a consistent, authentic brand voice while identifying and activating relevant cultural moments, contributing to content that resonates and drives engagement. Performance & Optimization - Define and track KPIs across platforms (e.g., engagement rate, reach, follower growth, video views, saves/shares), and own overall channel performance with a clear understanding of what is working and where to adjust. - Lead monthly and quarterly performance readouts, synthesizing key insights and translating them into actionable recommendations to continuously optimize content, cadence, and channel strategy. - Partner with paid media and influencer teams to amplify high-performing content and inform boosting strategy. Community & Agency Leadership - Oversee community engagement in partnership with our social agency, ensuring timely, on-brand interactions with the Simple Mills community (ie surprise & delights). - Leverage social listening and monitoring to track brand sentiment and identify emerging conversations, informing content and engagement opportunities. - Manage -day agency partnership, providing clear direction, feedback, and prioritization. - Monitor agency performance against defined scope and KPIs, identifying opportunities to improve effectiveness. - Perform other duties as assigned Simple Mills is a rapidly growing company on the forefront of the clean food movement, transforming center store grocery aisles with products made from nutritious, purposeful ingredients. Simple Mills is a female-founded business, started by Katlin Smith in 2012 out of her own Kitchen. In the company’s short history, Simple Mills has disrupted the market to become the #1 bestselling natural baking mix, the #1 bestselling natural cracker, and the #1 bestselling natural cookie. The company’s mission is to advance the holistic health of the planet and its people by positively impacting the way food is made. Simple Mills is distributed in 25, + stores nationally including Whole Foods, Target, Walmart, Kroger, Costco and more, and available online at retailers like Amazon and Thrive Market. The brand’s newest products include: Organic Seed Flour Crackers, Seed & Nut Flour Sweet Thins, and Soft-Baked Almond Flour Bars. Simple Mills has earned broad recognition from top tier media and industry associations- including a segment on our Founder & CEO Katlin Smith on NPR’s How I Built This. Our products have been featured in a wide array of top publications including Vogue, Women's Health, Forbes, Business Insider, mindbodygreen, NOSH and many more. Simple Mills was also a 2020 Health Magazine Snack Award Winner! Performance & Optimization - Define and track KPIs across platforms (e.g., engagement rate, reach, follower growth, video views, saves/shares), and own overall channel performance with a clear understanding of what is working and where to adjust. - Lead monthly and quarterly performance readouts, synthesizing key insights and translating them into actionable recommendations to continuously optimize content, cadence, and channel strategy. - Partner with paid media and influencer teams to amplify high-performing content and inform boosting strategy. Community & Agency Leadership - Oversee community engagement in partnership with our social agency, ensuring timely, on-brand interactions with the Simple Mills community (ie surprise & delights). - Leverage social listening and monitoring to track brand sentiment and identify emerging conversations, informing content and engagement opportunities. - Manage -day agency partnership, providing clear direction, feedback, and prioritization. - Monitor agency performance against defined scope and KPIs, identifying opportunities to improve effectiveness. - Perform other duties as assigned Simple Mills is a rapidly growing company on the forefront of the clean food movement, transforming center store grocery aisles with products made from nutritious, purposeful ingredients. Simple Mills is a female-founded business, started by Katlin Smith in 2012 out of her own Kitchen. In the company’s short history, Simple Mills has disrupted the market to become the #1 bestselling natural baking mix, the #1 bestselling natural cracker, and the #1 bestselling natural cookie. The company’s mission is to advance the holistic health of the planet and its people by positively impacting the way food is made. Simple Mills is distributed in 25, + stores nationally including Whole Foods, Target, Walmart, Kroger, Costco and more, and available online at retailers like Amazon and Thrive Market. The brand’s newest products include: Organic Seed Flour Crackers, Seed & Nut Flour Sweet Thins, and Soft-Baked Almond Flour Bars. Simple Mills has earned broad recognition from top tier media and industry associations- including a segment on our Founder & CEO Katlin Smith on NPR’s How I Built This. Our products have been featured in a wide array of top publications including Vogue, Women's Health, Forbes, Business Insider, mindbodygreen, NOSH and many more. Simple Mills was also a 2020 Health Magazine Snack Award Winner! Company Description Simple Mills is on a mission to transform the way food is made—creating nourishing products with astounding ingredients and amazing taste. Founded in 2012 by Katlin Smith, our brand has redefined center-aisle grocery by delivering better-for-you products made with unique, nutrient-dense ingredients. Today, we’re a category leader and the #1 natural brand in multiple segments, with distribution in over 30, stores nationwide. Now, as part of Flowers Foods, we’re entering an exciting new phase of growth—expanding our reach, accelerating innovation, and amplifying brand awareness, all while preserving the integrity of what makes Simple Mills special. Our commitment to using simple, high-quality ingredients ensures that every bite is both delicious and nutritious. Whether it’s watermelon seed flour in our cookies or butternut squash in our crackers, we craft our products with innovative, wholesome ingredients that deliver extraordinary taste. Our work has been recognized by Fast Company as a Brand That Matters and a Most Innovative Company, and has been featured in Vogue, Forbes, Women’s Health, and NPR’s How I Built This. In 2024, Inc. named us one of its Best Workplaces, reflecting our dedication to fostering an inspiring and inclusive environment where passionate individuals can make a difference. Join us as we shape the future of food. .simplemills.com. About the Organization A leading national non-profit organization in Saudi Arabia focused on vegetation development, ecosystem restoration, and combating desertification. The organization plays a strategic role in advancing environmental sustainability initiatives aligned with na
Chief Environmental Officer (CEO - Environment)
raw_description: About the Organization A leading national non-profit organization in Saudi Arabia focused on vegetation development, ecosystem restoration, and combating desertification. The organization plays a strategic role in advancing environmental sustainability initiatives aligned with national priorities. Role Overview The Chief Environmental Officer (CEvO) will serve as the senior executive responsible for shaping and leading the organization’s environmental strategy, programs, and impact. This role requires a visionary leader with deep technical expertise and strong execution capability to drive large-scale environmental initiatives across vegetation development, land restoration, and sustainability. The CEvO will work closely with executive leadership and stakeholders to ensure alignment with national environmental priorities and long-term sustainability goals. Key Responsibilities Strategic Leadership - Define and lead the organization’s environmental vision, strategy, and long-term roadmap. - Align environmental programs with national sustainability priorities and initiatives. - Provide executive oversight across all environmental and restoration programs. Program & Operational Oversight - Oversee large-scale vegetation restoration, land rehabilitation, and sustainability programs. - Ensure effective planning, execution, and performance of environmental initiatives. - Establish and monitor KPIs and impact metrics. Technical Authority - Act as the organization’s subject matter authority in environmental science, restoration ecology, and desertification control. - Review and approve scientific approaches, methodologies, and project frameworks. Stakeholder & Partnership Management - Build and maintain relationships with government entities, international organizations, and research institutions. - Represent the organization in high-level forums and environmental platforms. Governance & Compliance - Ensure adherence to environmental regulations, standards, and best practices. - Strengthen governance frameworks and reporting mechanisms. Team Leadership - Build, lead, and mentor a high-performing team of environmental experts and specialists. - Drive a culture of performance, accountability, and innovation. Ultimate Peaks is a premier executive search and workforce advisory firm, specializing in leadership hiring, strategic talent placement, and human capital consulting. We partner with businesses across Saudi Arabia and the GCC to build high-performing teams and optimize workforce strategies
description: The CEvO will work closely with executive leadership and stakeholders to ensure alignment with national environmental priorities and long-term sustainability goals. Ultimate Peaks is a premier executive search and workforce advisory firm, specializing in leadership hiring, strategic talent placement, and human capital consulting.

raw_description: About AllTrails AllTrails is the app for exploring the outdoors. We help people spend more time outside by connecting them to their adventure. AllTrails is home to the world’s largest outdoor community, with members and trails around the globe, and supports inclusive access to nature. Every day, we solve incredibly hard problems to get more people outside, for their wellbeing and the collective care of the natural world. Join us! This is a U.S. based remote position. While this role does not require in-person attendance, we have a strong preference for candidates based in or near one of these cities: San Francisco CA, Portland OR, Seattle WA, Denver CO, and New York NY. Being in one of these cities creates opportunities to connect with teammates through Trail Days, co-working sessions, and local gatherings while still having the flexibility to work remotely. San Francisco employees are highly encouraged to come to the office one day per week. About the role Reporting to the Manager, People & Talent Operations, this role will help build the infrastructure that supports the employee experience at AllTrails. As our People Operations Analyst, you’ll own and improve key People systems and processes across HRIS administration, compensation, analytics, data integrity, and cyclical People programs. This role is strongest for someone who has worked on a People Ops team before, brings strong systems and analytical skills, and has the judgment and follow-through to keep high-volume operational work accurate and moving. We encourage you to apply early. We will remove this post on Monday June 8th to ensure we can review all applications. About AllTrails AllTrails is the app for exploring the outdoors. All legitimate communication from our recruitment team will come from an official calstart.org email address via email, we will not text you about a role you have not applied to or shown interest in. About Us: CALSTART is a mission-driven industry organization focused on transportation decarbonization and clean air for all. For over 30 years, it’s been CALSTART’s mission to develop, assess, and implement large-scale, zero-emission transportation solutions to mitigate climate change and support economic growth. CALSTART works with businesses, organizations, governments, and communities to create real-life impact toward clean air and equitable access to clean transportation for all. CALSTART provides scientific, technical and policy support for regulatory development and clean technology and infrastructure acceleration. title: Digital Program Manager
pay_candidate_snippets: Salary commensurate with experience
raw_description: We are seeking an experienced and motivated individual to join Vocal Media's team! As a Digital Program Manager, you'll oversee -day operations of client social media programs, managing a dedicated team of social media managers while ensuring seamless program execution and client satisfaction. You'll serve as the primary operational lead for client accounts, balancing staff management, program logistics, client communication, and strategic guidance. This role requires strong leadership skills, operational excellence, and the ability to maintain high-quality deliverables while fostering team growth and client relationships. This is a full-time position through November 2026, with the opportunity for extension. Responsibilities Staff Management & Team Development Lead and develop a team of strategists to execute social media programs, providing regular coaching, feedback, and professional development opportunities to drive content excellence and team performance; Monitor content output and performance across client social media accounts, working closely with team members to track audience data, engagement metrics, and campaign effectiveness; Foster a positive, inclusive team culture by promoting collaboration, recognizing achievements, and motivating team members to perform at their best while maintaining high standards of work quality. Program Operations & Administration Manage program operations, including tracking budgets, analyzing content and audience trends, and hiring and onboarding social media managers; Collaborate with internal departments on administrative requirements and tools to execute and optimize programs. Client Relationship Management Produce comprehensive client-facing reports that showcase program performance, highlight key metrics, provide strategic insights, and demonstrate ROI across social media initiatives; Lead weekly client meetings, presenting program updates, facilitating strategic discussions, and serving as the primary point of contact for -day program needs; Maintain consistent and proactive communication with clients, responding to inquiries promptly and providing regular program updates that keep stakeholders informed and engaged This is a full-time position through November 2026, with the opportunity for extension

title: Meme Creator
pay_candidate_snippets: USD 1000 - 1000 per month
raw_description: We’re looking for meme creators who have their finger on the pulse of the internet — tracking, interpreting, and predicting trends before they hit the mainstream. These contract roles focus on developing and executing vertical video-first content strategies across TikTok, Instagram Reels, and other social platforms. The ideal candidate brings deep platform fluency, sharp trend awareness, and the ability to rapidly create compelling, shareable content on topics like, finance, and culture in a fast-paced, ever-evolving digital landscape. Responsibilities Time Commitment & Output Dedicate 10-12 hours weekly with flexible scheduling; Participate in two 30-min weekly check-ins about the project; Produce and manage 10-15 posts per account for 2+ accounts weekly across TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, and platforms. Content Development Source, identify, and clip relevant video content from outlets, cultural events, and social media platforms; Craft platform-optimized captions and post copy that authentically represents each account's distinct voice; Strategically leverage our comprehensive content database including stories, videos, photos, CapCut templates, and trending sounds. Strategy & Analysis Monitor emerging platform trends and algorithm updates to continuously refine content approach; Track and analyze key performance metrics and audience engagement patterns. Collaboration Work effectively with fellow team members and Content leadership; Contribute to cross-platform content initiatives and strategy discussions. raw_description: About GoodLeap: GoodLeap is a technology company delivering best-in-class financing and software products for sustainable solutions, from solar panels and batteries to energy-efficient HVAC, heat pumps, roofing, windows, and more. Over 1 million homeowners have benefited from our simple, fast, and frictionless technology that makes the adoption of these products more affordable, accessible, and easier to understand. Thousands of professionals deploying home efficiency and solar solutions rely on GoodLeap’s proprietary, AI-powered applications and developer tools to drive more transparent customer communication, deeper business intelligence, and streamlined payment and operations. Our platform has led to more than $30 billion in financing for sustainable solutions since 2018. GoodLeap is also proud to support our award-winning nonprofit, GivePower, which is building and deploying life-saving water and clean electricity systems, changing the lives of more than 1.6 million people across Africa, Asia, and South America. The Manager, Servicing Enablement, is responsible for overseeing the -day operational performance of third-party sub-servicers. title: Principal Product Manager, Fraud and Risk

raw_description: About GoodLeap: GoodLeap is a technology company delivering best-in-class financing and software products for sustainable solutions, from solar panels and batteries to energy-efficient HVAC, heat pumps, roofing, windows, and more. Over 1 million homeowners have benefited from our simple, fast, and frictionless technology that makes the adoption of these products more affordable, accessible, and easier to understand. Thousands of professionals deploying home efficiency and solar solutions rely on GoodLeap’s proprietary, AI-powered applications and developer tools to drive more transparent customer communication, deeper business intelligence, and streamlined payment and operations. Our platform has led to more than $30 billion in financing for sustainable solutions since 2018. GoodLeap is also proud to support our award-winning nonprofit, GivePower, which is building and deploying life-saving water and clean electricity systems, changing the lives of more than 1.6 million people across Africa, Asia, and South America. We're looking for a Principal Product Manager to drive the fraud and risk product function for our consumer products. This is a high-impact IC role responsible for architecting the systems, controls, and decisioning that protect our credit products, consumer payments, and homeowner experiences while maintaining a seamless, high-conversion user journey. You'll own the strategy and execution for defined fraud and risk domains, bringing clarity to complex challenges, aligning partners around the right signals and defenses, and personally driving roadmap initiatives that materially reduce losses. You operate with deep technical fluency and hands-on rigor, functioning as the subject matter expert across fraud vectors, risk modeling, and regulatory alignment. The ideal candidate brings systems-level thinking and the ability to operate with significant autonomy in a fast-moving fintech environment, doing the work, not just directing it GoodLeap is also proud to support our award-winning nonprofit, GivePower, which is building and deploying life-saving water and clean electricity systems, changing the lives of more than 1. title: Senior Software Engineer (Full-Stack)
pay_candidate_snippets: USA base pay range (CA, WA, NJ, MA, DC, NYC) per year: $144,900 - $164,500 USA base pay range (all other US states) per year: $131,700 - $149,500 In addition to the above salary, this role may be eligible for a bonus.
raw_description: About GoodLeap: GoodLeap is a technology company delivering best-in-class financing and software products for sustainable solutions, from solar panels and batteries to energy-efficient HVAC, heat pumps, roofing, windows, and more. Over 1 million homeowners have benefited from our simple, fast, and frictionless technology that makes the adoption of these products more affordable, accessible, and easier to understand. Thousands of professionals deploying home efficiency and solar solutions rely on GoodLeap’s proprietary, AI-powered applications and developer tools to drive more transparent customer communication, deeper business intelligence, and streamlined payment and operations. Our platform has led to more than $30 billion in financing for sustainable solutions since 2018. GoodLeap is also proud to support our award-winning nonprofit, GivePower, which is building and deploying life-saving water and clean electricity systems, changing the lives of more than 1.6 million people across Africa, Asia, and South America. Position Overview We are seeking a highly skilled Senior Full-Stack Software Engineer to bring technical excellence, initiative and leadership to our team. As a Senior Engineer, you’ll be a key contributor to the design, development, and scaling of our platform, working closely with product managers, designers, and fellow engineers to build solutions that are secure, reliable, and performance-optimized. You’ll work across the stack, you’ll lead initiatives from concept to delivery. This is a full-stack position with a strong emphasis on frontend development and close collaboration with the Product team. You’ll take the lead on major initiatives—planning features, defining MVPs, breaking projects into actionable phases, and helping to remove blockers for the team. This is an exciting opportunity for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment and is motivated to take ownership of high-impact work within a growing company GoodLeap is also proud to support our award-winning nonprofit, GivePower, which is building and deploying life-saving water and clean electricity systems, changing the lives of more than 1. title: Freelance Video Producer - Sports Coverage
raw_description: Position Title: Freelance Video Producer - Sports Coverage Job Type: Freelance/Independent Contractor Compensation: $3,000 for a full project, including creating and executing the 10-15 minute video Remote About MPU More Perfect Union is an advocacy, journalism, and education organization with a mission to build power for the working class. We cover politics, policy, labor, business, and economic through a class lens, and we pair our reporting with activism to help working people win policy fights. Launched in February 2021, More Perfect Union’s Emmy award-winning coverage has garnered over 1 billion video views and over 5 million followers and subscribers. About the Position More Perfect Union is expanding our coverage of sports stories reported through an economic justice lens. Issues we’re interested in include the monopoly power of sports governing bodies, efforts to organize players unions, the wealth and power of team owners and anything else that sits at the nexus of sports and corporate greed. We are looking for freelance video producers to create longform videos (10-15 minutes) on these topics for our YouTube channel and other social media platforms. For reference, here are some videos we’ve created in this lane: -- The NFL Is Choosing Concerts Over Player Safety. It’s Ending Careers. -- The Billionaire Family Ruining NASCAR | Ft. John Russell -- Can Sports Betting Be Fun Again? And videos from other channels we are inspired by: -- Why Saudi Arabia wants all the sports -- Architect Reveals How NFL Stadiums Are Engineered to Maximize Profit | WSJ Pro Perfected Ideal candidates will lead video projects from pitch to publish, overseeing all aspects of production, taking direction from senior More Perfect Union staff and working closely with other team members. Producers will be responsible for pitching and executing ideas related to sports that live within MPU’s mission of elevating working class issues and explaining complicated political and economic ideas to a broad audience through accessible YouTube formats. This role must be immersed in sports knowledge and excited about the process of telling those stories through video, with the technical and narrative skills to create powerful stories that hook viewers and keep people watching to the end. The position requires a high level of self-sufficiency, strong collaboration and communication skills, and a desire to help develop and grow our brand. Core Responsibilities: - Pitching ideas for new videos that fit within More Perfect Union’s brand and mission; - Developing, scripting and producing enterprise videos, primarily for YouTube - Sourcing original reporting and thorough research for your script - Casting interviewees, experts and on-camera talent for your pieces - Directing on-camera talent - Occasionally hosting videos as needed Qualifications: - Proficiency and understanding of all aspects of video production - Experience working on video explainers about sports, and the business of sports preferred - Strong reporting background, with work experience in journalism or digital media - Bonus for on-camera hosting experience - Field production experience –– setting up shoots, finding interviewees, working with videographers At More Perfect Union we strive to increase diversity, equity, inclusion and justice in all elements of our work. We recruit, employ, train, compensate, and promote regardless of race, religion, creed, national origin, ancestry, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, the uniformed services, or any other characteristic protected under applicable federal, state, or local law. We strongly encourage members of historically underrepresented and marginalized communities to apply.!! Important Notice!! To protect your privacy and ensure the legitimacy of our hiring process, please only respond to emails from our official domain, @perfectunion.us. We will never ask for personal information or payment during the recruitment process. Be cautious of any emails or job offers that do not come from this domain. title: Freelance Videographers
raw_description: Freelance Role: Videographers More Perfect Union is looking to expand our network of talented videographers to film for us. We film all over the United States and have shoots nearly every week. Who: We are looking for videographers from across the country and from diverse backgrounds who want to help make videos on worker power, labor, and economic justice. Specs: Our videos are filmed in 1080p, 24fps, and are generally shot in LOG. Our Class Room videos are filmed in 4k, 24fps, and are shot in LOG. Required Equipment: - High quality camera that can shoot in 1080p and 4k - Wireless lavalier microphones - Lighting equipment Compensation: For half-day shoots (5 hours or less), our budget is generally between $600-$800. For full-day shoots, (5.5 hours to 10 hours), our budget is generally between $1000-$1500. We also pay for mileage for shoots that are farther than 50 miles away using the IRS mileage rate. Full day shoots also receive a $16/per diem. If you are interested in being put on our roster of videographers, please fill out the below form and be sure to include your portfolio/reel. We will reach out when shoots arise in your area. Applications will only be reviewed when made using the above link.!! Important Notice!! To protect your privacy and ensure the legitimacy of our hiring process, please only respond to emails from our official domain, @perfectunion.us. We will never ask for personal information or payment during the recruitment process. Be cautious of any emails or job offers that do not come from this domain Specs: Our news videos are filmed in 1080p, 24fps, and are generally shot in LOG. Our Class Room videos are filmed in 4k, 24fps, and are shot in LOG. Required Equipment: - High quality camera that can shoot in 1080p and 4k - Wireless lavalier microphones - Lighting equipment Compensation: For half-day shoots (5 hours or less), our budget is generally between $600-$800. 5 hours to 10 hours), our budget is generally between $1000-$1500. Specs: Our videos are filmed in 1080p, 24fps, and are generally shot in LOG. Our Class Room videos are filmed in 4k, 24fps, and are shot in LOG. Required Equipment: - High quality camera that can shoot in 1080p and 4k - Wireless lavalier microphones - Lighting equipment Compensation: For half-day shoots (5 hours or less), our budget is generally between $600-$800. For full-day shoots, (5.5 hours to 10 hours), our budget is generally between $1000-$1500. We also pay for mileage for shoots that are farther than 50 miles away using the IRS mileage rate. Full day shoots also receive a $16/per diem. If you are interested in being put on our roster of videographers, please fill out the below form and be sure to include your portfolio/reel. We will reach out when shoots arise in your area. Applications will only be reviewed when made using the above link.!! Important Notice!! To protect your privacy and ensure the legitimacy of our hiring process, please only respond to emails from our official domain, @perfectunion.us. We will never ask for personal information or payment during the recruitment process. title: Chief of Staff
raw_description: Recognized on the [2025 “TIME100 ” list]( PowerLines is rapidly becoming a source and leader in informing the national discussion of this issue and has already shaped the conversation in the [New York Times]([Washington Post]( [Wall Street) Journal]( 116f061d1a706432163510c51bdd65f3), [Politico]([Bloomberg]( [CBS ]( [NPR]( [Marketplace]( [Financial Times]( [TIME]( [Vox]( [The Daily Mail]( and [MIT Technology) Review]( -manage-power-outages-with-ai/). PowerLines is in a period of significant organizational growth and seeks a full-time Chief of Staff to directly support its Founder and Executive Director. The Chief of Staff will act as a force multiplier for leadership and the organization by operating as an “air traffic controller” by supporting operational execution of strategy, managing up and across the organization, and streamlining internal processes and clearing bottlenecks. This will not be a static, slow-paced role. The person in this role will be expected to work at a rapid pace

title: State Impact Specialist (Wisconsin)

raw_description: This is a remote position based in Wisconsin. The priority application deadline for this role is March 8, 2026. The Impact Specialist will build on Protect Democracy’s recent related advocacy efforts across states, ranging from educating the public and legal audiences as well as legislative advocacy to improve Wisconsin elections. The Impact Specialist will be responsible for executing policy initiatives, maintaining and expanding relationships with key stakeholders, and serving as an expert representative of the organization to diverse audiences. Implement multi-pronged strategic campaigns aimed at influencing key political and civic leaders in Wisconsin to rebuild our democratic norms and institutions that are under threat

title: Director, Business Development (Remote, US)
raw_description: **Who We Are** Renew Home is on a mission to change how we power the world by making it easier for customers to save energy and money at home as part of the largest residential virtual power plant in North America. We partner with industry-leading brands to better manage residential energy for users by prioritizing efficiency, savings, and comfort — and cleaner energy for everyone. We are an. We strongly encourage candidates to check out our website at [www.renewhome.com]( to about the world-changing work we are doing. **Role Summary** The Director of Business Development at Renew Home will be an exceptional strategic leader focused on identifying and developing new growth opportunities, with a primary emphasis on the electric vehicle (EV) sector and a secondary emphasis on the battery storage sector. This is a senior individual contributor role that requires a highly experienced and varied B2B business development toolkit. The primary focus will be on forging new strategic partnerships, exploring new markets, and developing innovative business models. **What You Will Do** - **Strategic Opportunity Development:** Identify, evaluate, and develop a pipeline of “zero-to-one” opportunities in the EV and battery storage markets. Lead all stages – from market analysis and initial concept through partnership development and execution. - ** -Market Evaluation:** Develop and utilize financial, market, and energy models to assess new business opportunities, evaluate market strategies, and structure complex partnerships, particularly those involving battery storage and EV integration. - **Executive Engagement & Thought Leadership:** Build and maintain strong relationships with key decision-makers at EV manufacturers, managed charging providers, residential storage suppliers, and other strategic partners. **Who We Are** Renew Home is on a mission to change how we power the world by making it easier for customers to save energy and money at home as part of the largest residential virtual power plant in North America. We strongly encourage candidates to check out our website at [www.renewhome.com]( to about the world-changing work we are doing. **Role Summary** You'll lead the Infrastructure team — a group of 5-8 engineers responsible for the foundation everything else at Renew Home runs on. The team owns cloud accounts, networking, security, CI/CD, deployment tooling, observability, and the developer experience that surrounds all of it. Other engineering teams build on top of what your team provides — your team's leverage shows up in how fast the rest of the company can ship. This is a hands-on engineering manager role. You'll spend most of your time on people, planning, and cross-team alignment, but you should be technical enough to debate architecture, review a tricky design doc, and sense when a project is in trouble before it lands on your desk. **What You Will Do** - The team. Hiring, growth, performance, and career development for seven engineers across three sub-disciplines. Setting expectations, giving direct feedback, and building a team people want to stay on. - The roadmap. Translating company priorities into an infra roadmap that balances reliability work, platform investment, security posture, and the steady stream of "unblock the product team" asks. - Operational health. Participate in on-call rotations, On-call rotation quality, incident response, postmortem culture, and the SLOs to keep the platform healthy. - Cross-team partnership. You'll be the primary point of contact for product engineering, data, and security leadership. Your team's reputation lives or dies by how well it serves its consumers. - Security posture. Partnering with security engineering to drive identity, access, audit, and compliance work — making sure security is built into the platform rather than bolted on. - CI/CD and test automation. The health of the pipelines, runners, and test infrastructure the entire engineering org relies on. Partnering with your automation engineers to drive build times down, flake rates down, and developer confidence up. - Budget and vendor relationships. Cloud spend, tooling decisions, and the -build calls that compound over time. **Who We Are** Renew Home is on a mission to change how we power the world by making it easier for customers to save energy and money at home as part of the largest residential virtual power plant in North America. title: Staff Engineer, Full Stack (Remote, US)
raw_description: We partner with industry-leading brands to better manage residential energy for users by prioritizing efficiency, savings, and comfort — and cleaner energy for everyone. We strongly encourage candidates to check out our website at [www.renewhome.com](to about the world-changing work we are doing. ** ** - Lead Technical Design:) You will lead the architectural design for complex software projects, covering both the backend systems and the frontend user interfaces. - Build & Integrate: You'll build and maintain features, bridging the gap between our backend infrastructure and the frontend user experience. - Mentor & Lead: You will guide small groups of engineers, mentor team members, and help support a strong engineering culture. - Collaborate: You'll work closely with other teams, including product, design, and others. - Ensure Quality: You will be responsible for overall code quality, software best practices, and ensuring a high-quality user experience. - Our Tech: You'll work with technologies like TypeScript, React, Python, FastAPI, and SQL databases (like PostgreSQL) on cloud platforms (AWS/GCP). - You’ll help support a strong engineering culture
